NCT03521934.name: SOLOIST-WHF
NCT03521934.group: T2D + recent worsening HF hospitalisation, sotagliflozin 200-400 mg/day vs placebo (CV death / HF hosp / urgent HF visit HR)

allOutcomes.title: Total CV death, HF hospitalisation, or urgent HF visit (primary, HR)

NCT03521934.snippet: Source: Bhatt DL et al. NEJM 2021;384:117-128 (SOLOIST-WHF; sponsor-truncated early).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,222 adults with type 2 diabetes who had been hospitalised for worsening heart failure (LVEF any, started during or within 3 days after hospitalisation) randomized 1:1 double-blind to sotagliflozin 200 mg/day (up-titrated to 400 mg) or matched placebo. Trial truncated early at median 9-month follow-up due to sponsor loss-of-funding, not for efficacy/safety.

evidence.label: Primary - Total CV Events
evidence.text: Total events of CV death, HF hospitalisation, or urgent HF visit: sotagliflozin 245 vs placebo 355; rate 51.0 vs 76.3 per 100 patient-years; hazard ratio 0.67 (95% CI 0.52-0.85; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met. Benefit was rapid (curves separated within 28 days).

evidence.label: Secondary - Components and Safety
evidence.text: HF hospitalisation HR 0.64 (0.49-0.83); urgent HF visit HR 0.46; CV death HR 0.84 (non-significant due to early truncation). Diarrhoea, genital mycotic infections, volume depletion more common with sotagliflozin; severe hypoglycaemia and DKA rare.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by LVEF. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 99% vital status. D4 LOW - centrally adjudicated endpoints. D5 SOME CONCERNS - early truncation with protocol amendment for revised primary endpoint (total events vs time-to-first).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 85 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria:~* Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.~* Admitted to the hospital, or urgent heart failure visit for worsening heart failure.~* Prior diagnosis of heart failure (> 3 months).~* Prior chronic treatment for heart failure with a loop diuretic (eg furosemide, torsemide, bumetanide) for > 30 days.~* Randomized when hemodynamically stable, prior to hospital discharge or within 3 days of discharge.~* Brain natriuretic peptide (BNP) ≥150 picograms per milliliter (pg/mL) (≥450 pg/mL for parti...

NCT03315143.name: SCORED
NCT03315143.group: T2D + CKD stage 3-4 with CV risk factors, sotagliflozin 200-400 mg/day vs placebo (CV death / HF hosp / urgent HF visit HR)

allOutcomes.title: Total CV death, HF hospitalisation, or urgent HF visit (primary amended, HR)

NCT03315143.snippet: Source: Bhatt DL et al. NEJM 2021;384:129-139 (SCORED; sponsor-truncated early).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 10,584 adults with type 2 diabetes, CKD (eGFR 25-60 mL/min/1.73m^2), and additional CV risk factors randomized 1:1 double-blind to sotagliflozin 200 mg/day (up-titrated to 400 mg) or matched placebo. Trial truncated early at median 16-month follow-up.

evidence.label: Primary - Total CV Events
evidence.text: Total events of CV death, HF hospitalisation, or urgent HF visit (amended primary after truncation): sotagliflozin 400 vs placebo 540 events; rate 5.6 vs 7.5 per 100 patient-years; hazard ratio 0.74 (95% CI 0.63-0.88; P<0.001). Primary met. Original primary (MACE) also numerically reduced.

evidence.label: Secondary - HF, Kidney, Safety
evidence.text: HF hospitalisation or urgent HF visit HR 0.67 (0.55-0.82). CV death HR 0.90 (NS). Albuminuria reduction favoured sotagliflozin. Diarrhoea, genital mycotic infections more common; DKA rare (0.6% vs 0.3%); no eGFR dip requiring stop. Amputation and Fournier gangrene risks balanced.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by eGFR and region. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 99% vital status. D4 LOW - centrally adjudicated endpoints. D5 SOME CONCERNS - early truncation with amended primary endpoint definition.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. title: RapidMeta Cardiology | Sotagliflozin (Dual SGLT1/2 Inhibitor) in HF / CKD-T2D v1.1
